content:
✨ ccpocket | Dart
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling developers to remotely control and interact with AI coding agents (like Codex and Claude) from their mobile devices or desktops, facilitating a more flexible and accessible development workflow.
✨ Key Features: • Control Codex and Claude coding agents from mobile and desktop • Remote session management and action approval • Cross-device context persistence
📖 Summary: CC Pocket is a mobile and desktop application that allows users to control coding agent sessions (Codex and Claude) remotely via a self-hosted WebSocket bridge server. It enables users to start, manage, and approve agent actions from their phone, tablet, or desktop, offering a seamless coding experience across devices.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 docker-pi-hole | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a simple and reliable way to deploy Pi-hole as a network-wide ad blocker using Docker.
✨ Key Features: • Official Pi-hole Docker image • Network-wide ad blocking • DNS sinkhole functionality • Easy deployment with Docker Compose • Customizable environment variables for configuration
📖 Summary: This repository provides the official Docker image for Pi-hole, a network-wide ad blocker and DNS sinkhole. It allows users to easily deploy Pi-hole in a containerized environment for efficient ad blocking across their entire network.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 mgrep | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a natural language interface for searching and retrieving information from local files and the web, particularly useful for developers and AI agents.
✨ Key Features: • Natural-language search • Semantic, multilingual, and multimodal search capabilities • Built-in web search (--web) • Background indexing with `mgrep watch` • Friendly device-login flow
📖 Summary: mgrep is a command-line tool that enables semantic searching across various file types, including code, text, and PDFs, using natural language queries. It features background indexing, web search integration, and is designed for both human and agent use, aiming to reduce token usage for AI agents.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 letube-backend | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a robust and scalable backend infrastructure for a video hosting and live streaming platform.
✨ Key Features: • Video hosting service • Live streaming • VOD (Video on Demand) • HLS adaptive streaming • FFmpeg transcoding
📖 Summary: LeTube Backend is a microservices-based backend for a video hosting platform, supporting both Video on Demand (VOD) and live streaming. It leverages HLS for adaptive streaming, FFmpeg for transcoding, and S3-compatible storage for media files, with RabbitMQ for asynchronous task processing.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 BRING | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Developing and playing in persistent, AI-driven fantasy role-playing games with rich narrative and intelligent NPCs.
✨ Key Features: • Layered World Building (L1, L2, L3) • Graph-First Knowledge Management with self-healing graph • Self-Optimising Memory (FAISS-accelerated, time-based partitioning) • Deterministic Probability System for actions • Advanced Romance and Birth/Isekai systems
📖 Summary: BRING v2 is a production-ready, AI-powered platform for creating persistent fantasy worlds with interactive roleplaying. It combines generative world building, graph-based knowledge management, and LLM-driven narrative orchestration with FAISS-accelerated memory for intelligent NPCs and dynamic storytelling.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 GitOS | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Developing a custom, open-source operating system from the ground up through community contributions.
✨ Key Features: • Custom Bootloader (NASM Assembly) • Modular Kernel (C++) • Minimalist Console UI • Community-driven development
📖 Summary: GitOS is an open-source operating system project built collaboratively by a global community. It features a custom bootloader written in NASM assembly and a modular kernel developed in C++, aiming for a minimalist, console-based user interface.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
